codeReview.summary: The DetailModal component is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage. It successfully displays detailed coverage information for detected/manual items, handles all three coverage statuses (covered, conditional, not_covered), and implements all required closing mechanisms (X button, backdrop click, Escape key). The component properly handles camera feed dimming, policy change updates, and focus trap accessibility. 42 comprehensive tests cover open/close behavior, all coverage status types, keyboard accessibility, and edge cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The DetailModal component follows the established component conventions: PascalCase .jsx files, co-located test files, @/ path alias imports, and Tailwind utility classes. No convention gaps found.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/DetailModal/DetailModal.jsx follows all patterns documented in AGENTS.md

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the frontend-worker skill procedures correctly. The implementation includes proper TDD approach (42 tests before/during implementation), React hooks usage, and integration with existing context. All validation contract requirements (VAL-DM-001 through VAL-DM-007) are fulfilled.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows all verification commands passed (vitest, lint, dev server). Tests cover all validation contract scenarios.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly uses the coverage lookup utility and coverageRules.json for dynamic content. This shows proper understanding of the Coverage Rules Engine documented in architecture.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: DetailModal.jsx imports lookupCoverage from @/utils/coverageLookup.js and dynamically generates content based on category + policyType combination.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The modal correctly handles the camera vs dashboard entry point distinction via the 'source' property on item objects, enabling different backdrop opacity (60% for camera to keep feed visible, 50% for dashboard). This edge case was properly identified and implemented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/DetailModal/DetailModal.jsx line 178: `const isFromCamera = item.source === 'camera'` and line 204: `className={\`absolute inset-0 ${isFromCamera ? 'bg-black/60' : 'bg-black/50'}\`}

summary: The detail-modal feature is fully implemented and ready for user testing. The component displays comprehensive coverage information for all three status types (covered, conditional, not_covered), supports multiple closing mechanisms (X button, backdrop click, Escape key), maintains camera feed visibility when opened from camera view, updates content on policy changes, and implements proper accessibility with focus trap and ARIA attributes. 42 tests provide thorough coverage of all functionality including edge cases. The implementation correctly integrates with CoverageOverlay (bounding box clicks) and Dashboard (item row clicks) as required by VAL-OVERLAY-010 and VAL-CROSS-007.
